# AC Verification Script: [Story Title]

<!--
  PURPOSE: Plain-language walkthrough of every AC in this story.
  
  AUDIENCE: Anyone — BA, QA lead, PM, domain expert, or developer.
  No technical knowledge required to use this script.
  
  WHEN TO USE:
  - Before /definition-of-ready: human expert confirms behaviour is correct
    before the coding agent implements it
  - After merge: smoke test confirming what shipped matches what was specified
  - During sprint review: structured demo script
  
  HOW TO USE:
  Work through each scenario in order. For each step, do exactly what is described.
  Record Pass / Fail and any notes. Do not interpret — if the behaviour differs 
  from the expected outcome, even slightly, record it as a finding.
  
  Generated by: /test-plan skill alongside the technical test plan.
  This script covers the same ACs as the test plan but in human terms.
-->

**Story reference:** [link to story artefact]
**Technical test plan:** [link to test plan artefact]
**Script version:** 1 (update version if ACs change post-implementation)
**Verified by:** [name] | **Date:** [date] | **Context:** [ ] Pre-code  [ ] Post-merge  [ ] Demo

## Scenarios

<!--
  One scenario per AC, plus edge cases.
  Each scenario: numbered steps → expected outcome → pass/fail field.
  
  Language rules:
  - Use "click", "type", "press" — not "navigate to", "interact with", "utilize"
  - Name UI elements exactly as they appear on screen
  - Expected outcomes describe what you see/hear/receive — not what the system "should do"
  - If an outcome involves a file, describe what's in the file
  - If an outcome involves a message, quote the exact message text
-->

---

## Findings
